steps.title: Use the point at zero
steps.content:
Since $$(0,-\sqrt{363})$$ is on the graph,
$$h(0)=-\sqrt{c}=-\sqrt{363}$$
Thus $$c=363$$

steps.title: Use the zero at three
steps.content:
Since $(3,0)$ is on the graph, the expression under the radical must be $0$ when $$x=3$$
$$3^2+3b+363=0$$
$$9+3b+363=0$$
$$b=-124$$

steps.title: Find the other zero
steps.content: Now $$h(m)=0$$ means $$m^2-124m+363=0$$ One solution is $$m=3$$ By the product of roots, $$3m=363$$ $$m=121$$ The answer is 121.
